revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Hododa-Kofungruppe]][[en:Hodota Kofun Cluster]][[ja:保渡田古墳群]]
{{Short description|Three Kofun period Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Hodota Kofun Cluster | native_name =保渡田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =CKT20111-C13-18 Kamitsukenu Haniwano-sato Park.jpg; File:Hodota-kofungun Hachimanzuka-kofun-1.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Hodota Kofun Cluster | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hodota Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|22|51|N|138|59|09|E|region:JP|display=inline}}; {{Coord|36.378556|N|138.983583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Takasaki|qid=Q336438|1=Takasaki}}| region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of =; {{ill|Kamitsukenu Haniwano-sato Park|qid=Q21652793|1=Kamitsukenu Haniwano-sato Park}}; {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun (Kasugai)|qid=Q11371448|1=Futagoyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Hachimanzuka-kofun|qid=Q17218702|1=Hachimanzuka-kofun}}| length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Hodota Kofun Cluster'''|保渡田古墳群|}} is a group of three [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in what is now the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1985.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/171665/1|title=保渡田古墳群|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== This group of Kofuns is distributed at the southern foot of {{ill|Mount Haruna|en|Mount Haruna|qid=Q1191690|lt=Mount Haruna|1=Mount Haruna}}, and were constructed from the end of the latter half of the 5th century to the first half of the 6th century AD. All are {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The Futakoyama Kofun is both the largest and oldest of the group and is located to the southwest. The Hachimanzuka Kofun is in the northeast, and the Yakushizuka Kofun, which is the youngest, is in the northwest. Information on excavated items and archaeological sites is displayed at the neighboring {{nihongo|'''Kamitsuki-no-sato Museum of Archaeology'''|かみつけの里博物館|Kamitsuki-no-sato Hakubutsukan}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
===Futagoyama Kofun === The {{nihongo|'''Futagoyama Kofun'''|二子山古墳|}} is constructed in three tiers and has an overall length of 108 meters. The bottom two tiers were covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found on top and on the terrace between the two tiers. Other types of ''haniwa'' shaped as human figures, horses and other animals, and houses, etc. were also found. The {{ill|Tumulus|en|Tumulus|qid=Q34023|lt=Tumulus|1=Tumulus}} was surrounded by a double horseshoe-shaped moat, and there are four smaller {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} dome-shaped tumuli within the inner moat, flanking the posterior circular portion of the main tumulus of all sides. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is located at the center of the apex of the circular portion of the tumulus, and consists of a river stone-lined pit-type room with a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The tomb has been robbed in premodern times and the lid of the sarcophagus is missing. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} missed by the robbers included a decorative iron sword, gilt-bronze shoes, iron farm implements and stone imitations of everyday objects and other ornaments. Currently, the chamber is backfilled for preservation.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 108 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 74 meter diameter x 10 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 71 meter width x 7 meters high
=== Hachimanzuka Kofun === The {{nihongo|'''Hachimanzuka Kofun'''|八幡塚古墳|}} has an overall length of 102 meters, but its original height is uncertain since the top of the tumulus has been shaved off. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were found on top . Other types of ''haniwa'' shaped as human figures, horses and other animals, and houses, etc. were also found, and of special interest was a ''haniwa'' in the shape of a {{ill|Cormorant|en|Cormorant|qid=Q3901247|lt=Cormorant|1=Cormorant}} with a fish in its beak. The practice of {{ill|Cormorant fishing|en|Cormorant fishing|qid=Q1784476|lt=Cormorant fishing|1=Cormorant fishing}} was documented in the Chinese {{ill|Book of Sui|en|Book of Sui|qid=Q1156924|lt=Book of Sui|1=Book of Sui}} from the same period as the Kofun period in Japan, and this was the first physical evidence corroborating the Chinese history. The tumulus was surrounded by a double horseshoe-shaped moat, the inner dike of which also had ''fukiishi''. As with the Futagoyama Kofun, four smaller dome-shaped tumuli are located within the inner moat, flanking the circular portion of the main tumulus of all sides. The Hachimanzuka Kofun was also robbed in antiquity, and the burial chamber has collapsed. Fragments of iron swords and other weapons, fragments of armor and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 102 meters ;Posterior circular portion:56 meter diameter x 6 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 53 meter width
=== Yakushizuka Kofun === The {{nihongo|'''Yakushizuka Kofun'''|薬師塚古墳|}} is thought to have had an overall length of over 100 meters, and to have been of two-tied construction with ''fukiishi'' and rows of cylindrical ''haniwa'', with a double moat. However, a large portion of the tumulus was destroyed with the construction of an adjacent Buddhist temple and its graveyard. The tumulus was excavated in 1683, and the stone boat-shaped sarcophagus remains on the mound. Some of the surviving grave goods are stored at the temple of Saiko-ji,<ref name="Shiseki"/> and are now {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9633|title=上野国保渡田薬師塚古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> Hodota-kofungun Hachimanzuka-kofun-1.JPG| Hachimanzuka Kofun Hodota-kofungun Hachimanzuka-kofun sekishitsu.JPG|Hachimanzuka Kofun sarcophagus Hodota-kofungun Futagoyama-kofun.JPG| Futagoyama Kofun Hodota-kofungun Yakushizuka-kofun.JPG|Yakushizuka Kofun Kamitsukeno-sato Museum of Archaeology.jpg|Kamitsuke-no-sato Museum of Archaeology </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|qid=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}

==External links== ]]]]
